pages.title: Media Center
pages.subtitle: The Media Center produces and publishes audiovisual materials that highlight the Institute's efforts and its partner network in advancing urban development practices across the Arab world.

items.title: Institute Director Participates in Al-Futtaim Dialogue on Urban Development
items.description: The Director General of the Arab Urban Development Institute participated in a dialogue session organized by Al-Futtaim Group on the future of urban development.
body: The Director General of the Arab Urban Development Institute participated in a dialogue session organized by Al-Futtaim Group, attended by leading experts and decision-makers in urban development.
The session discussed key challenges facing Arab cities amid global transformations, and ways to strengthen public-private partnerships for sustainable urban development.
The Institute emphasized the importance of exchanging expertise among Arab cities and its role as a regional platform supporting urban planning and policies.

items.title: UAE Urban Contractors League Opens with Municipal Leadership
items.description: The UAE Urban Contractors League launched with broad participation from municipal leaders and urban development specialists.
body: The UAE Urban Contractors League launched with broad participation from municipal leaders and specialists in urban development.
The league aims to enhance quality practices in the construction sector and exchange expertise among Arab cities in infrastructure and urban planning.

items.title: Institute Holds Annual Arab Municipal Cooperation Network Meeting
items.description: The Institute held its annual meeting of the Arab Municipal Cooperation Network to discuss strengthening integration among Arab cities.
body: The Arab Urban Development Institute held its annual meeting of the Arab Municipal Cooperation Network, with representatives from more than 650 Arab cities.
The meeting focused on strengthening cooperation mechanisms among Arab municipalities and exchanging best practices in municipal governance and sustainable urban development.

items.title: New Initiative to Support Sustainable Urban Planning in Arab Cities
items.description: The Institute announced a new initiative to strengthen Arab municipalities' capacities in sustainable urban planning.
body: The Arab Urban Development Institute announced a new initiative to strengthen Arab municipalities' capacities in sustainable urban planning.
The initiative includes specialized training programs, workshops, and knowledge exchange platforms among Arab cities in planning and urban policy.

items.title: Workshop on Good Municipal Governance with International Experts
items.description: The Institute organized a specialized workshop on good municipal governance with experts from across the Arab region.
body: The Institute organized a specialized workshop on good municipal governance with experts and specialists from across the Arab region.
The workshop addressed mechanisms for enhancing transparency and accountability in municipalities, and the role of technology in improving municipal services.

items.title: Urban Development Conference 2025 Discusses the Future of Arab Cities
items.description: The Institute hosted the Urban Development Conference with leading experts and decision-makers discussing key challenges and opportunities in Arab cities.
body: The Arab Urban Development Institute hosted the Urban Development Conference 2025, with leading experts and decision-makers from across the Arab region.
The conference addressed multiple themes including urban resilience, local economic development, municipal governance, and innovation in urban policy.

intro.description: A bi-monthly publication in Arabic and English, serving as a communication channel for key issues and topics related to urban development in Arab cities.

items.title: Green Infrastructure Towards an Integrated Green System in Arab Cities
body: This issue of Mudununa explores green infrastructure as a fundamental pillar of sustainable urban development in Arab cities.
The issue reviews successful experiences from Arab cities in urban greening, green space management, and integrating green infrastructure with urban planning.

items.title: The City as a Destination for Urban Tourism in the Arab Region
body: This issue focuses on urban tourism as one of the drivers of local economic development in Arab cities.
It includes interviews with urban tourism experts and case studies from Arab cities that have successfully developed distinctive urban tourism products.

items.title: Solid Waste Management in Arab Cities
body: This issue addresses the challenges of solid waste management in Arab cities and innovative solutions adopted by some municipalities.
It reviews best practices in waste sorting, recycling, and the transition towards a circular economy in Arab cities.

items.title: Smart Cities: Opportunities and Challenges in the Arab World
body: This issue explores the concept of smart cities and its applications in the Arab context.
It includes analyses of smart city policies and experiences of Arab municipalities in using technology to improve municipal services.

items.title: Urban Resilience: How Arab Cities Prepare for Crises
body: This issue focuses on the concept of urban resilience and its importance in facing climate, social, and economic challenges.
It provides a practical framework for Arab municipalities to enhance their capacity to respond to and recover from crises.

items.title: Local Economic Development in Arab Cities
body: This issue addresses local economic development strategies in Arab cities.
It reviews successful models for supporting small and medium enterprises and promoting urban entrepreneurship.

intro.description: A series of monthly meetings organized by the Arab Urban Development Institute, aimed at exchanging expertise and knowledge in urban development and planning.

items.title: Smart and Sustainable Lighting for Future Urban Environments
body: The Institute organized the nineteenth City Movement meeting on smart and sustainable lighting for future urban environments.
The meeting discussed the latest technologies in sustainable urban lighting and their impact on quality of life in cities, with presentations from Arab and international cities.

items.title: City Resilience: Building Smart Cities Capable of Facing Crises
body: This meeting discussed the concept of city resilience and building smart cities capable of facing various crises.
Experts from different specializations participated to provide insights on enhancing urban resilience in Arab cities.

items.title: How Far is the City from its Residents? The Cognitive City and the 15-Minute Index: A Reading of the Riyadh Region Municipality Experience
body: The Institute held the twentieth City Movement meeting on the cognitive city and the 15-minute index, with a reading of the Riyadh Region Municipality experience.
The meeting discussed the 15-minute city concept and how to measure proximity of services to residents, showcasing the Riyadh experience as a model for Arab cities.
Prof. Greg Clark, Dr. Anas AlMugairi, and Dr. Riyadh Al Shaikh participated, exchanging views on the future of urban planning in the region.

items.title: Good Urban Governance: Experiences and Practices from Arab Cities
body: This meeting addressed good urban governance, showcasing successful experiences from Arab cities in enhancing transparency and municipal accountability.

items.title: Localizing the Local Economy: Development Opportunities in Arab Cities
body: The meeting discussed strategies for localizing the local economy in Arab cities and the role of municipalities in supporting local projects and SMEs.

items.title: Climate Adaptation: Urban Policies for Arab Cities
body: This meeting focused on climate adaptation policies in Arab cities, presenting action plans from leading municipalities in this field.

items.title: The Secretary-General on the Future of Arab Cities
items.description: The Secretary-General's vision on the challenges and opportunities for Arab cities.
body: Arab cities are undergoing rapid transformations that require an integrated development vision.
The institute emphasizes the importance of sustainable urban planning.
